_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 1710 batch)
_meta.note:
VA RICHMOND LOCAL batch — 5 officials (4 Richmond city/school + 1 Suffolk CC). reva-trammell (Richmond CC D8 Southside, D, since 2007, re-elected Nov 5 2024), sarah-abubaker (Richmond CC D4 Southwest, I-progressive, sworn Jan 2 2025), shavonda-fernandez (Richmond RPS School Board Chair D9, nonpartisan, appointed Feb 2024 + elected Nov 2024 + Chair Jan 5 2026), stephanie-lynch (Richmond CC D5 Central, D, since 2020 special election + re-elected Nov 2024), ebony-wright (Suffolk CC Sleepy Hole Borough, D-aligned via 2024 DNC delegate, sworn Jan 2 2025, first Black woman on Suffolk Council). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Trammell — HARD TRUE sanctity_of_life q1: ONLY Richmond councilmember who refused to co-sponsor the 2022 post-Dobbs abortion-protection resolution (every other member + mayor signed). HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: most aggressive tax-cutter on council, repeatedly proposed 4-cent rate cuts in 2022/2024/2025; YES on Oct 2025 rate-cut to $1.16. HARD TRUE public_justice q0: AFFIRMATIVE pro-police advocacy ('breaks my heart to see what is going on... they are being belittled, how they are being cussed out' July 2020); co-proposed police+firefighter pay raises 2022; chair Public Safety Committee. HARD TRUE public_justice q1: voted NO on 2020 defund-adjacent RPD budget analysis. HARD TRUE self_defense q0: voted to STRIKE non-lethal weapon ban in 2020 committee; 2019 ordinance dissent ('would not prevent those intent on doing harm'). HETERODOX D — RCDC declined to endorse her 2024; she won anyway by personal brand. (2) Abubaker — HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: one of 3 YES votes on Oct 2025 $1.16 rate cut ('we have to give people back their money'); voted NO on FY26 budget; publicly attacked Avula CAO Donald's 'unelected office publicly stating they are going to defy the will of a body that was elected.' Fix party Unknown→I. (3) Fernandez — HARD FALSE self_defense q1: Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction 2024. STRONG HETERODOX FAITH PROFILE: ordained Elder UN Church International, Liberty University grad — explicit Christian identity unusual for progressive-endorsed RPS chair. Pragmatic-fiscal posture (closed RVA, FY27 deficit cuts). Most culture-war RPS votes (2022 trans, 2021 SRO) PRE-DATE her tenure → null not FALSE. (4) Lynch — HARD FALSE sanctity_of_life q1: voted unanimously to approve PP Northside clinic July 2024. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: YES on Oct 2025 rate cut + introduced rate-cut resolution + 'lighting money on fire' quote. HARD FALSE public_justice q0: June 2020 'transformative rebalancing of... institutions in our public safety and criminal justice system' Facebook post in support of diverting RPD funds. HARD FALSE public_justice q1: co-patroned Marcus Alert + RPD reallocation resolution w/ Mike Jones. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: 2021 Stoney 'gun violence as public health crisis' declaration. HARD FALSE self_defense q1: Council $80K gun-buyback funding with Lynch on Council, no dissent. Genuine heterodox catch on tax restraint. (5) Wright — VERY THIN ~17mo record. MIXED economic_stewardship: FY26 May 2025 voted YES (7-1) hold the rate at $1.07 producing effective ~4.71% tax increase via reassessment (sole NO from Tim Johnson) — rubric-adverse; FY27 May 2026 herself MOVED approval of 1¢ rate cut to $1.06 — rubric-favorable. Two-way pull → null per discipline. Suffolk Council collectively opposed Wilroy Rd ICE office siting Apr 2026 but Wright did not individually lead; null per discipline. Suffolk 2A Constitutional City Dec 2019 + strengthened Jan 2020 BOTH pre-date her (sworn Jan 2 2025) → N/A. Confirmed D-aligned via 2024 DNC delegate selection by Suffolk Democratic Committee. Navy vet + DOJ/DOD federal civil servant background. Fix party Unknown→D.

RUNBOOK BLOCKER STATUS (this batch CONTINUES the deliberate 1630-precedent departure — deferred 4 records that the picker keeps surfacing): (a) rick-west VA Mayor of Chesapeake — 6TH consecutive defer; slug-collision with rick-west OK State Rep persists, refine-records.py by_slug last-wins; research stashed in 2026-06-02 0950 dossier; would score 67/100 with 6 cited cells. NEEDS slug-rename helper (proposed rename: rick-west VA → rick-west-chesapeake; preserve OK record). (b)+(c)+(d) vernon-tillage + william-dodson + yolanda-thomas — already research-complete with confidence=roster_verified per 1625 Portsmouth batch; runbook picker uses 'not confidence.startswith("evidence")' filter which excludes roster_verified, so they keep being surfaced. NEEDS filter widening to accept roster_verified as 'done' OR promote-to-evidence_local-with-all-nulls convention. Both blockers should be addressed by a one-time engine fix before the next ~5 LOCAL VA batches. Picked next 5 fresh candidates (4 Richmond — finishing the Richmond CC/SB sweep — + 1 Suffolk start) to avoid burning 4 of 5 batch slots on blocked records.

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no church affiliation found on her campaign profile, council bio, or media coverage reviewed.
profile.education: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED in formal degree terms; lifelong Southside Richmond resident.
profile.background: Reva Trammell — Richmond City Council District 8 (Southside Richmond). Among the longest-serving members on Richmond Council, in office since January 1, 2007 (multi-decade tenure including prior 2002-2008 service). Re-elected Nov 5, 2024 over Frank Wilson Jr.; current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. Heterodox-D populist 'people's advocate' on Southside issues. The Richmond City Democratic Committee (RCDC) declined to endorse her in 2024 — she won anyway on personal brand. Known for anti-tax-hike rhetoric, vocal pro-police voice, and twice leading the (failed both times) Richmond casino referendum push despite progressive opposition.

reva-trammell.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 8 (Southside), in office since Jan 2007 (multi-decade tenure); re-elected Nov 5 2024 over Frank Wilson Jr.; current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. HETERODOX-D CATCH (the biggest find this batch): the ONLY Richmond councilmember who refused to co-sponsor the 2022 post-Dobbs abortion-protection resolution — every other member + Mayor Stoney signed, Trammell did not (vpm.org 2024-07-23 Planned Parenthood land-transfer context). HARD TRUE sanctity_of_life q1: that 2022 refusal is the cleanest cross-party signal on the rubric's sanctuary-city-for-abortion inverse direction. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: most aggressive tax-cutter on Council — repeatedly proposed 4-cent rate cuts from $1.20 to $1.16 in 2022, 2024, and 2025; in Oct 2025 she was one of only 3 YES votes for the cut with Abubaker and Lynch (vpm.org 2025-10-15 + richmonder.org). Multi-cycle restraint signal that crosses the Avula-administration / Newbille leadership line. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared, AFFIRMATIVE): July 2020 vote against police budget reassignment analysis — 'Our police officers, I mean it breaks my heart to see what is going on... they are being belittled, how they are being cussed out, how they're being told F the police' (wric.com). Co-proposed 2022 police+firefighter pay raises ($1.9-$4M) with Newbille and Robertson. Chair of Public Safety Committee. RPD publicly credited her for the Jeff Davis corridor cleanup. THIS IS THE HIGH-BAR cell that requires affirmative advocacy — Trammell clearly clears it (parallel to but stronger than the federal pro-police caucus signal). HARD TRUE public_justice q1: voted NO on the 2020 RPD-budget-reassignment-to-mental-health-services analysis — a defund-adjacent measure; voted to strike the non-lethal weapon ban in 2020 committee (richmondfreepress.com 2020-07-30 + vpm.org 2020-09-29). HARD TRUE self_defense q0: abstained on the 2019 Richmond ordinance banning guns on city property/parks, stating the ordinance 'would not prevent those intent on doing harm from doing so' and objecting to process (wtvr.com 2019-07-01); voted to STRIKE a 2020 non-lethal weapon ban in committee (vpm.org 2020-09-29). Strike-vote is the affirmative action that clears the rubric on q0. NOT scored biblical_marriage cells: no public Trammell-individual record on Pride proclamations (Richmond's joint proclamations have been Mayor + Council Pres + VP signatories, not full council), no public position on drag-in-libraries / trans / SOGI located — null per discipline. NOT scored christian_liberty cells: no public Trammell-individual record. NOT scored election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration: no quote-bearing Trammell-individual votes / statements surfaced. CONFEDERATE MONUMENTS NOTE (not scored — no rubric cell): initially voted NO three times before reversing to support removal — populist constituent-driven not ideological. 5 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells across sanctity_of_life (1), economic_stewardship (1), public_justice (2), self_defense (1) — a heterodox-D council member with a coherent populist/pro-cop/tax-cutter brand that genuinely deviates from the Richmond progressive machine on multiple vectors. This is the cleanest cross-party catch in the Richmond batches so far.

1.note: Of all Richmond councilmembers + Mayor Stoney, Trammell was the ONLY one who did NOT co-sponsor the 2022 post-Dobbs city resolution 'to protect and expand access to safe and legal reproductive health care.' Every other member signed on; she abstained from sponsorship. The cleanest cross-party signal on the rubric's pro-life / refuse-sanctuary-for-abortion direction in the 2026 Richmond batches.

2.note: Most aggressive tax-cutter on Council — repeatedly proposed lowering Richmond's real estate tax rate from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 in 2022, 2024, and 2025. In Oct 2025 was one of only 3 YES votes for the cut with Abubaker and Lynch (failed). Multi-cycle restraint signal that consistently crosses the Avula admin / Newbille leadership hold-the-rate coalition.

0.note: HIGH BAR cleared — AFFIRMATIVE pro-police advocacy in own voice. July 2020 vote against police budget reassignment analysis: 'Our police officers, I mean it breaks my heart to see what is going on... they are being belittled, how they are being cussed out, how they're being told F the police.' Co-proposed 2022 police+firefighter pay raises ($1.9-$4M) with Newbille and Robertson. Chair of Public Safety Committee. RPD publicly credited her for the Jeff Davis corridor cleanup. Rubric-favorable on q0 (affirmative pro-police, not just budget-funding inertia).

1.note: Voted NO on the 2020 resolution to analyze the RPD budget for reassignment to mental-health/social-services — a defund-adjacent measure. Voted to strike the 2020 non-lethal weapon ban in committee. Anti-defund / anti-decarceral signal (rubric-favorable on q1).

0.note: Abstained on the 2019 Richmond ordinance banning guns on city property/parks, stating the ordinance 'would not prevent those intent on doing harm from doing so' and objecting to the process. Voted to STRIKE a 2020 non-lethal weapon ban in committee. Strike-vote is the affirmative action that clears the rubric on local q0 (opposes local gun ordinances / pro-2A).

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY PROFESSED — no public faith profession on her campaign site, VCU/Wilder profiles, or press coverage reviewed in either direction. (Surname 'Abubaker' is associated with Arabic/Muslim heritage and her father is a Libyan immigrant, but she has made NO public Islamic, Christian, or other faith profession; treat as not a publicly-professed Christian for q4-style cells.)
profile.education: M.P.A., Virginia Commonwealth University, Wilder School of Government and Public Affairs.
profile.background: Sarah Abubaker — Richmond City Council District 4 (Southwest Richmond). Director of Strategic Communications at Collegiate School (private). Registered as Independent on the ballot but explicitly characterized as progressive by the Virginia Progressive Voters Guide and endorsed by outgoing progressive Councilor Kristen Nye. Ran UNOPPOSED in the Nov 5 2024 general election, winning ~97.65%. Sworn in Jan 2 2025; first term began Jan 1 2025; current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. Father is a Libyan immigrant.

sarah-abubaker.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 4 (Southwest), sworn Jan 2 2025 (ran unopposed Nov 2024); current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: no public faith profession in either direction; treat as not a publicly-professed Christian. PARTY: Independent ballot label, progressive-aligned via Progressive Voters Guide endorsement + Nye succession. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: October 2025, Abubaker was one of 3 councilors voting to lower the real estate tax rate from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (with Trammell and Lynch). In her own voice: 'we have to give people back their money'; framed $1.20 as 'one of the highest in the Commonwealth' (vpm.org 2025-09-23 + richmondfreepress.com 2025-10-16 + wric.com 2025). Voted NO on FY26 budget as 'protest of process, not policy'; pushed budget-process overhaul (vpm.org 2025-05-13). Publicly attacked the Avula CAO Donald's refusal to budget for lower rates as 'people who are in unelected office publicly stating they are going to defy the will of a body that was elected' (richmonder.org). Three-vector heterodox-progressive fiscal-restraint signal — this is a genuinely unusual catch on the rubric for a Progressive-Voters-Guide-endorsed urban I/D-aligned council member. NOT scored sanctity_of_life cells: no individually-attributed Abubaker vote / quote located; she has not been on Council long enough to have a Dobbs-resolution record (sworn after the 2022 vote). NOT scored biblical_marriage q4: Richmond's June 10 2025 'Joint Proclamation' was signed by Mayor Avula + Council Pres Newbille + VP Jordan — Abubaker is on Council but is NOT a named signatory, and silence-by-non-dissent is not an affirmative endorsement under strict discipline → null not FALSE. NOT scored family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense: no quote-bearing individually-attributed Abubaker positions located across the 17 months she has been seated. PARTY FIX: Unknown → I (per the ballot label). 1 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells across economic_stewardship — coherent left-of-D-machine fiscal-restraint catch on an otherwise lightly-documented first-term progressive Independent. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget cycle and any post-election cultural-issue council votes.

2.note: Oct 2025: one of 3 YES votes (with Trammell and Lynch) to lower the real estate tax rate from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100. In her own voice: 'we have to give people back their money'; called $1.20 'one of the highest in the Commonwealth.' Voted NO on FY26 budget as 'protest of process, not policy.' Publicly attacked Avula CAO Donald's refusal to budget for lower rates as 'people who are in unelected office publicly stating they are going to defy the will of a body that was elected.' Multi-vector heterodox-progressive restraint signal.

profile.religion: Ordained Elder (2014), United Nations Church International, Richmond (Bishop Orrin K. Pullings) — Black Pentecostal congregation. Founder of 'Jesus Groupie Clothing' (2003). Liberty University graduate degree. Strong outward Christian identity ('sinner saved by grace' platform language, weekly biblical 'Live Chats'). UNUSUALLY explicit Christian profession for a progressive-endorsed RPS member.
profile.background: Shavonda R. Dixon-Fernandez (filed as 'Shavonda Dixon'; now goes by 'Shavonda Fernandez' post-marriage). Richmond Public Schools (RPS) School Board, District 9 (Southside Richmond). Appointed Feb 5 2024 (unanimous board vote to fill the seat vacated when Nicole Jones moved to City Council); elected to a full term Nov 5 2024 unopposed (~98%); sworn in for the elected term Jan 8 2025 during the Richmond water crisis; elected School Board CHAIR on Jan 5 2026 by unanimous roll-call (nominated by Matt Percival, who became vice-chair). Next election Nov 2028. RPS board races are nonpartisan.

shavonda-fernandez.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond Public Schools School Board District 9 (Southside), appointed Feb 5 2024 + elected Nov 5 2024 + elected Chair Jan 5 2026; current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: ORDAINED ELDER (2014) at United Nations Church International (Bishop Orrin K. Pullings, Black Pentecostal); founder of 'Jesus Groupie Clothing' (2003); Liberty University graduate degree; weekly biblical 'Live Chats.' HARD FALSE self_defense q1: accepted the 2024 Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction as 9th District School Board candidate — an AFFIRMATIVE anti-2A endorsement she chose to publicize on her platform (ballotpedia.org Shavonda_Dixon). This is the clearest hard-negative on the rubric for this candidate. CRITICAL NOT-SCORED public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): the 2021 5-4 SRO modification vote pre-dates her Feb 2024 appointment; RPS currently has 12 SROs plus unarmed Care & Safety Associates; NO EVIDENCE FOUND of Fernandez voting to expand, defund, or restore SROs. After the May 2026 John Marshall graduation incident she called it an 'isolated incident' and did NOT call for added police presence (yahoo.com news video). NOT scored biblical_marriage q2 / family_child_sovereignty q2: the 2022 RPS rejection of Youngkin transgender model policies (8-1, Doerr's motion) PRE-DATES her Feb 2024 appointment (newsweek.com 2022-10-03), so she is NOT recorded on that vote → null not FALSE. NO subsequent revisit located. NOT scored economic_stewardship cells: she voted with the unanimous board on FY27 budget cuts (~$4M deficit close) including teacher-stipend cuts and closing Richmond Virtual Academy June 2026 — pragmatic-fiscal posture but unanimous board action does not isolate her individual restraint signal → null per discipline. NOT scored christian_liberty cells: her outward Christian profession is REAL and documented in profile, but no individually-attributed Fernandez vote on prayer / Christian-club / religious-exemption located → null per strict cite-only discipline (profile religion field captures this honestly). NOT scored sanctity_of_life / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / public_justice q1-q4 / self_defense q0/q2: no individually-attributed Fernandez positions located. HETERODOX FAITH PROFILE NOTE: she is one of the most explicitly Christian-identified progressive-endorsed urban school-board members in the 2026 Richmond batches — classic Black Christian Democrat archetype, conservative on personal morality language while progressive on policy alignments. Recommend revisiting after any 2026-2027 culture-war RPS vote (library content, parental notification, SRO modifications). 0 TRUE / 1 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — driven by data scarcity in most cells (she missed the 2021 SRO and 2022 trans-policy votes by appointment timing), not abundant evidence of opposition.

1.note: Accepted the 2024 Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction as 9th District School Board candidate — an AFFIRMATIVE anti-2A endorsement she chose to publicize. Direct gun-control endorsement on the rubric-adverse direction (q1).

profile.background: Stephanie Lynch — Richmond City Council District 5 (Central Richmond / Fan / Carytown area). Licensed clinical social worker (LCSW). Joined Council via a 2020 special election after Parker Agelasto resigned; re-elected Nov 5 2024; current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. Endorsed by Progressive Voters Guide 2024. Known for progressive priorities (mental health, housing, 'violence prevention'). Vocal voice on the 2nd Street Connector, casino opposition (revenue/schools-funding grounds rather than moral), and Mayor Stoney–era policy.

stephanie-lynch.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 5 (Central), since 2020 special election, re-elected Nov 5 2024; current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. HARD FALSE sanctity_of_life q1: Lynch voted with the UNANIMOUS July 2024 Council vote to approve the Brook Hill School site land transfer to the Virginia League for Planned Parenthood for a $6M Northside health center (richmondbizsense.com 2024-07-23). Introduced resolution asking the administration to base its next budget on $1.16. In her own voice: paying current rates is 'lighting money on fire' (vpm.org 2025-10-15). NOT scored economic_stewardship q1 (debt restraint): no specific debt-restraint vote. HETERODOX CATCH NOTE: the tax-restraint signal is GENUINE and crosses the urban-progressive grain — she paired it with a worker-pay-floor resolution and FY26 budget criticism, so the restraint posture is real but not anti-spending overall. 1 TRUE / 5 FALSE on cited cells = coherent urban-progressive D council member with one genuine cross-party catch on fiscal restraint and dominant FALSEs on policing, guns, and Planned Parenthood facilitation.

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY PROFESSED — bio mentions support for 'faith-based, and volunteer organizations' as a generic phrase; no personal current-faith profession on record.

profile.background: Ebony Wright — Suffolk City Council, Sleepy Hole Borough (northern Suffolk: Bennetts Creek / Driver / Harbor View). U.S. Navy veteran (4 yrs active duty); federal civil servant at DOJ/DOD; founder of a Suffolk-schools scholarship nonprofit. FIRST BLACK WOMAN ever to sit on Suffolk City Council. Defeated 3-term incumbent Roger Fawcett 53.01%–46.53% in the Nov 5 2024 general election; sworn in Jan 2 2025; current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. Party: Suffolk Council races are nominally nonpartisan, but the Suffolk Democratic Committee elected her to represent Suffolk CD-2 as a delegate to the 2024 DNC National Convention (Aug 2024) — Democratic-alignment is unambiguous. A prior 'Wright for Suffolk School Board – Ebony' VPAP committee suggests an earlier school-board bid.

ebony-wright.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Suffolk City Council, Sleepy Hole Borough, sworn Jan 2 2025 after defeating 3-term incumbent Roger Fawcett 53.01-46.53% in the Nov 2024 general; current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: no public current-faith profession; bio mentions 'faith-based, and volunteer organizations' generically. PARTY: nominally nonpartisan Council race, but Suffolk Democratic Committee selected her as a 2024 DNC delegate (Suffolk CD-2) — D-alignment is unambiguous. PARTY FIX: Unknown → D. THIN ~17-MONTH RECORD: she has been seated since Jan 2 2025 and most rubric cells lack a quote-bearing individually-attributed position. ECONOMIC_STEWARDSHIP q2 — TWO-WAY MIXED SIGNAL, RESOLVED TO NULL PER DISCIPLINE: (a) FY26 budget (May 2025): voted YES (7-1) to keep the rate at $1.07/$100, which produced an effective ~4.71% tax increase via reassessment; sole NO was Cm. Tim Johnson who proposed a 2-cent cut. Wright said the budget was 'necessary to catch up on needs' unless a 'defined way' to lower it was shown (whro.org 2025-05-08 + suffolknewsherald 2025-05-07) — rubric-adverse direction. (b) FY27 budget (May 2026): Wright herself MOVED approval of the ~$954.8M budget with a 1-cent cut to $1.06; passed unanimously (wtkr.com 2026) — rubric-favorable direction. Two votes that pull opposite directions across consecutive budget cycles → null per strict cite-only discipline (neither vote in isolation cleanly clears the rubric, and the FY26 hold-the-rate + FY27 1¢ cut is a wash). REFUSE_STATE_OVERREACH / BORDER_IMMIGRATION — collective Council opposition to the proposed Wilroy Rd ICE office (April 2026) is documented (wavy.com + bluevirginia.us 2026-06 + whro.org 2026-04-24), with the City sending letters to large-property owners signaling opposition; Wright DID NOT individually lead this — Cm. Leroy Bennett was the visible voice. Per strict discipline, collective inaction without individual attribution → null not FALSE. SELF_DEFENSE: Suffolk passed its 7-0 'Constitutional City' 2A resolution Dec 18 2019 and the strengthened amendment Jan 15 2020 — BOTH pre-date Wright's Jan 2 2025 swearing-in (suffolknewsherald.com 2019-12-18 + 2020-01-15); she has not moved to repeal or weaken it. NOT scored — she was N/A on the relevant vote. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice cells — no individually-attributed Wright positions located in 17 months of service. PUBLIC_JUSTICE q0: campaign language 'comprehensive approach to public safety that maximizes the impact of public funds' and 'support first responders' is generic-supportive boilerplate, NOT affirmative advocacy — fails the HIGH BAR per discipline → null.
